meaning: n.粒状的,木纹状的,多粒的
examples: GRAININESS – the sand-like or granular appearance of an image.
Graininess becomes more pronounced with faster film and the degree of enlargement.
It has shown that any underlying quantum 'graininess' of space must be at much smaller scales than previously predicted.
And he has, although this may be an illusion produced by the graininess of the image, a dimple in his chin.
There was a graininess to the air, which had itself warmed and grown windless, as it always did before a storm.
